summary:
Claim: Insulin resistance in liver disease primarily results from hepatic steatosis.  

Evidence: - Intracellular triglyceride accumulation impairs insulin signaling by inhibiting IRS-1 activation and blocking the PI3K/Akt cascade. - Experimental data consistently link fatty liver (NAFLD/NASH) with defective glucose metabolism due to lipotoxicity.  

Answer: B

Answer: B. Answer choice: B
